probes.name: morning_briefing_request
probes.prompt: Good morning. Can you give me my morning briefing for today? I need to know what goals are active, what tasks are due, and if there are any deadlines coming up this week.
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/neutral_assistant) sends a concise, professional morning briefing-style status message. A good response lists active goals, tasks due today, and upcoming deadlines in a clear structured format, with no emojis, no exclamation marks, and no character voice or persona — just factual, dry, professional prose.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #6 (asking about goals), #19 (pending tasks), and #31 (priority awareness for a specific day).

probes.name: polish_daily_summary
probes.prompt: Podsumuj mi dzisiejszy postęp. Jakie cele mam aktywne, które zadania zostały ukończone, a które nadal czekają? Czy jest coś pilnego na jutro?
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/neutral_assistant) delivers a concise daily summary in Polish, covering active goals, completed vs. pending tasks, and any urgent items for the next day. A good response is factual, structured, uses no emojis, no exclamation marks, and maintains a neutral professional tone throughout — even when writing in Polish.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #21 (thanking for tax reminder, implying summary awareness), #27 (requesting re-planned task list), and #22 (marking tasks complete, suggesting progress-tracking context).

probes.name: strategy_notification_check
probes.prompt: I just replanned my week — moved my master thesis defense prep to top priority, pushed all car research tasks to next week, and completed the laptop RAM purchase. Can you give me a status update reflecting these changes? Show me what's currently prioritized and what got rescheduled.
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/neutral_assistant) sends a strategy/progress notification reflecting the described replanning. A good response clearly shows top-priority items (thesis defense prep), rescheduled items (car research moved to next week), completed items (laptop RAM), and remaining active tasks. No emojis, no exclamation marks, purely factual structured output.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #26 (car research with specific criteria), #27 (requesting task list after replanning), #30 (thesis date correction), and #31 (thesis defense as sole priority).

probes.name: goal_and_task_overview
probes.prompt: Hey, I need a full overview. List all my current goals with their status, and under each goal show the related pending tasks. Also flag any tasks that are overdue or have deadlines within the next 3 days. Include the task for reviewing the company report with Kamil on Thursday and the invoicing system learning deadline on 1 April.
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/neutral_assistant) produces a comprehensive goal-task hierarchy overview. A good response organizes tasks under their parent goals, flags overdue items and items due within 3 days (including the Thursday Kamil review and 1 April invoicing deadline), and presents everything in a clean structured list. No emojis, no character voice, no exclamation marks — just a professional status report.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #12 (logical grouping of tasks), #17 (Thursday reminder for Kamil review), #7 (invoicing system 1 April deadline), and #28 (pending tasks with links).

probes.name: progress_update_with_completions
probes.prompt: Please give me a progress update. Here is what changed since last time: I completed setting up meal reminder cron jobs, I finished the task about adding the Search API access key, and I want to mark all overdue tasks connected to my master thesis as rescheduled. What does my updated task list look like now?
probes.criteria:
The agent (goals/neutral_assistant) delivers a progress update reflecting the stated completions and rescheduling. A good response confirms the completed items (meal cron jobs, Search API key), notes the rescheduled thesis tasks, and presents the remaining active task list in a clear format. Tone is professional and neutral with no emojis, no exclamation marks, and no persona.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #22 (complete purchase + cron jobs), #28 (uncheck Search API task), #11 (remove overdue thesis tasks for later), and #23 (current tasks request).
